mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward returns in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output that supports near-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with a p-value greater than 0.10 during identified recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.5 percent annualized growth rate, equity markets anticipate restrictive monetary policy adjustments that compress valuation multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 55% of months where CPI growth breaches the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative information coefficient over 20-day horizons in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows or returns are negative in greater than 50% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that immediately erodes equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances following the two-month decline threshold.
